title: Peristeria stapelioides
intro: Peristeria stapelioides, also known as the Orchid of the Passion or the Stelis orchid, is a species of orchid native to Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, thanks to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Peristeria stapelioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Peristeria stapelioides is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Peristeria stapelioides is classified within the family Orchidaceae and further within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This species belongs to the genus Peristeria. The genus Peristeria is native to Central and South America. It is a type of orchid known for its showy and fragrant flowers. Peristeria stapelioides is also known as the "Dove Orchid" due to its unique flower shape.
